label: neither
rationale: 'Wacha wakikuyu wachukue' - permissive electoral observation about Kikuyu vote, no contempt or attack on the group.

label: hate
rationale: 'send those ugly luo wives of them back to luo land or in the afterlife' - collective contempt plus a death-adjacent expulsion framing ('in the afterlife') directed at Luo people as a group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus have made a MISTAKE' and 'It will TAKE Kikuyus 100 yrs to GET BACK into govt' - collective punishment framing that holds the entire Kikuyu community responsible for a political act and declares them unfit for power, NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Factual remark about a payment incident in Wamunyoro referencing Kipsigis; no contempt or attack on the group.

label: neither
rationale: 'Waluhya wote wanasema wako kwa Wamunyoro' is electoral bloc observation; 'Mole' is a personal insult at a named individual, not transferred onto the Luhya community.

label: hate
rationale: 'fucking jews are the problem world wide' - collective attribution of global harm to Jewish people as a group, plain NCIC-standard ethnic contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Gachagua is a TRIBALIST' - personal political insult aimed at one named politician, not transferred onto his community.

label: neither
rationale: 'Kalenjins in Wamunyoro to Ruto's profile' and 'Nabii hatakikani nyumbani' - electoral observation about Kalenjin sentiment toward Ruto, no contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Mzee', 'mjinga', 'mwizi' aimed at a named individual; 'tribal goons' refers to an individual's political network, not an ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'fundamental Muslims they will not honor any treaty any contract any agreement... with a gentile or a Jew' - collective immutable negative characteristic attributed to Muslims as a group.

label: neither
rationale: Cynical political commentary about government impunity and electoral fraud; 'Mta do?' is rhetorical resignation, no ethnic target or contempt.

label: neither
rationale: 'Luos have been the MOST shocked' - observation about a community's political reaction, no contempt or attack.

label: neither
rationale: Intra-community political intrigue commentary; 'tabia ya uchawi' is a personal moral criticism aimed at an individual's behaviour, not the Mulembe/Luhya nation as a group.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral arithmetic mocking the strategic logic of 'Luos voting for Ruto to punish murima'; no ethnic contempt, target is the voting decision.

label: offensive
rationale: 'More foolish ten times than Kalonzo, go to hell' - crude personal insult at a named individual with no ethnic dimension.

label: hate
rationale: 'Your tribesmen stole Raila's victory in 2007' holds the Kikuyu community collectively responsible for electoral theft and 'we are yet to put you where you belong' implies retributive intent against the group.

label: hate
rationale: Denies ethnic identity to named politicians - 'si mluhya, ni mnandi', 'mkikuyu' - to delegitimise their standing within the community, amounting to collective ethnic contempt and exclusion framing.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Greatest fool of all time, total idiot' - personal abuse of a named politician, no protected characteristic.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Evil creature' aimed at a named individual (Kasongo), no ethnic group targeted.

label: hate
rationale: 'All Kikuyus watolewe from powerful positions' - calls for collective exclusion of an ethnic community from government office, a textbook NCIC expulsion/exclusion case.

label: neither
rationale: Rhetorical question about cowardice at Wamunyoro; 'paka' is a personal taunt at an individual, no ethnic group attacked.

label: neither
rationale: Quotes and condemns Gachagua's statement ('Primitive tribalist Rigathi Gachagua says...'); the author's stance is condemnation - 'DCP is a moral cancer to the Kikuyu community.'

label: hate
rationale: 'Wantam Kikuyus' with 'waijnga' (fools) collectively attributes stupidity to the Kikuyu community as a group, NCIC ethnic contempt even without a violence call.

label: neither
rationale: 'Nobody wants anything to do with Kikuyu president or Kikuyu project' is harsh political criticism of a political project associated with an ethnic bloc, directed at a named politician's leadership; stops short of attributing a collective negative characteristic to Kikuyus as a people.

label: neither
rationale: 'Kasongo is the enemy' - personal political characterisation of a named individual, no ethnic dimension.
